Question: Determine whether the ordered pair is a solution to the system $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + y = 0 \\ x + 2 y = -5\end{matrix}\right . . \\ \end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓐ $$( 1 , -3 )$$ ⓑ $$( 0 , 0 )$$

Determine whether the ordered pair is a solution to the system $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 3 y = -8 \\ - 3 x - y = 4\end{matrix}\right . . \\ \end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓐ $$( 2 , -2 )$$ ⓑ $$( -2 , 2 )$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 3 y = -3 \\ x + y = 5\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}- x + y = 1 \\ 3 x + 2 y = 12\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}- x + y = 1 \\ 2 x + y = 10\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x + y = 6 \\ x + y = 1\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y = - \frac{1}{4} x + 2 \\ x + 4 y = - 8\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y = 3 x - 1 \\ 6 x - 2 y = 6\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y = - 3 x - 6 \\ 6 x + 2 y = - 12\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by graphing: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y = \frac{1}{2} x - 4 \\ 2 x - 4 y = 16\end{matrix}\right . .$$

Without graphing, determine the number of solutions and then classify the system of equations. ⓐ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y = -2 x - 4 \\ 4 x + 2 y = 9\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓑ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + 2 y = 2 \\ 2 x + y = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: ⓐ no solution, inconsistent, independent ⓑ one solution, consistent, independent

Question: Without graphing, determine the number of solutions and then classify the system of equations. ⓐ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y = \frac{1}{3} x - 5 \\ x - 3 y = 6\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓑ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + 4 y = 12 \\ - x + y = 3\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: ⓐ no solution, inconsistent, independent ⓑ one solution, consistent, independent

Question: Solve the system by substitution: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}- 2 x + y = -11 \\ x + 3 y = 9\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by substitution: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x + y = -1 \\ 4 x + 3 y = 3\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by substitution: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 4 y = -4 \\ - 3 x + 4 y = 0\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by substitution: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x - y = 0 \\ 2 x - 3 y = 5\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + y = 5 \\ 2 x - 3 y = 7\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x + y = - 5 \\ - 2 x - 2 y = - 2\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x - 4 y = - 9 \\ 5 x + 3 y = 14\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve each system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}7 x + 8 y = 4 \\ 3 x - 5 y = 27\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve each system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}\frac{1}{3} x - \frac{1}{2} y = 1 \\ \frac{3}{4} x - y = \frac{5}{2}\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve each system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + \frac{3}{5} y = - \frac{1}{5} \\ - \frac{1}{2} x - \frac{2}{3} y = \frac{5}{6}\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}5 x - 3 y = 15 \\ y = - 5 + \frac{5}{3} x\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system by elimination: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + 2 y = 6 \\ y = - \frac{1}{2} x + 3\end{matrix}\right . .$$

For each system of linear equations decide whether it would be more convenient to solve it by substitution or elimination. Explain your answer. ⓐ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x - 5 y = -32 \\ 3 x + 2 y = -1\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓑ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x = 2 y - 1 \\ 3 x - 5 y = -7\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: ⓐ Since both equations are in standard form, using elimination will be most convenient. ⓑ Since one equation is already solved for x , using substitution will be most convenient.

Question: For each system of linear equations decide whether it would be more convenient to solve it by substitution or elimination. Explain your answer. ⓐ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y = 2 x - 1 \\ 3 x - 4 y = - 6\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓑ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}6 x - 2 y = 12 \\ 3 x + 7 y = -13\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: ⓐ Since one equation is already solved for y , using substitution will be most convenient. ⓑ Since both equations are in standard form, using elimination will be most convenient.
